title: Postfit uncertainty band size in analyses with enormous number of events

posts.name: Peter Fackeldey
posts.text:
Dear combine experts,
we observe a large postfit uncertainty band compared to the stat. unc. of the data in some of our control regions in the scope of our semileptonic HH → bbWW analysis (see attached slides). Our analysis is very inclusive and deals with an enormous amount of events (see slide 2), which are categorised using a physics process multi-classification (DNN).
We have the following questions for you:

Is the observation of a large postfit uncertainty band compared to the stat. unc. of the data worrisome here?
Are you aware of other analysis with such a large number of events? If yes, is this a known behaviour?

Thank you very much,
Peter for the bbWW analysis team
20221013-fackeldey-cmsTalk-Postfit-Uncertainty-Band.pdf (700.2 KB)

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Can you tell us a little more about the systematics in the analysis?
Are you using toys or hessian? how (anti) correlated are the nuisances affecting the background predictions in those regions?
-Andrea
